 {"id":"bd-8op0","title":"Define ntm workflow pipeline for github-review","description":"Task: Define ntm Workflow Pipeline for github-review

Purpose
-------
Create the github-review.yaml workflow that orchestrates the multi-step
review process through ntm workflow engine.

Background: ntm Workflows
-------------------------
ntm supports YAML-defined workflows with:
- Sequential and parallel steps
- Agent (Claude) steps with prompts
- Shell steps for scripting
- Health checks and timeouts
- Question queuing with priorities
- Input/output variables

Workflow Location
-----------------
~/.config/ntm/workflows/github-review.yaml

Workflow Schema
---------------
schema_version: "2.0"
name: github-review
description: |
  Automated GitHub issue and PR review workflow (Plan Mode).
  Agent produces local patches + review-plan.json artifact.
  NO direct GitHub mutations.

inputs:
  worktree_path: (required) Path to isolated worktree
  repo_name: (required) GitHub repo identifier
  repo_digest_path: (optional) Cached digest path
  work_items: (required) JSON array of items to review

settings:
  timeout: "45m"
  on_error: "fail"
  notify_on_error: true

Steps
-----

1. verify_prerequisites (shell)
   - Check gh auth status
   - Verify worktree exists
   - Create .ru directory

2. understand_codebase (agent)
   - Read AGENTS.md and README.md
   - Load or create repo digest
   - Update digest if needed
   - Timeout: 10m
   - Health check every 30s

3. review_issues_prs (agent)
   - Main review work
   - Use gh for READ only
   - Create commits for fixes
   - Produce review-plan.json
   - Queue questions with priority
   - Timeout: 30m
   - Wait for user interaction

4. finalize_artifacts (shell)
   - Verify review-plan.json exists
   - Validate JSON structure
   - Log completion

Outputs
-------
  plan_path: Path to review-plan.json
  digest_path: Path to repo-digest.md
  items_reviewed: Count of items reviewed

Question Handling
-----------------
on_question:
  action: queue
  priority: from question urgency
  metadata:
    repo: repo_name
    worktree: worktree_path

This routes questions to ru TUI for aggregation.

Error Handling
--------------
- on_failure: abort (for prerequisites)
- on_failure: warn (for finalize)
- Health checks detect stalls
- Timeout prevents runaway sessions

Testing
-------
- Dry run workflow validation
- Step execution order
- Question queuing works
- Timeout handling
- Error propagation

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Workflow file validates
- [ ] All steps execute in order
- [ ] Questions queued correctly
- [ ] Timeouts enforced
- [ ] Artifacts produced","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:48.226320831-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:30:52.487839208-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:30:52.487839208-05:00","close_reason":"Added ntm workflow template in examples + doc pointer","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8op0","depends_on_id":"bd-k1kx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.753511995-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-8q3s","title":"Phase 3: Session Driver & Spawning","description":"# Phase 3: Session Driver & Spawning

## Objective
Implement a unified session driver interface that works with or without ntm, providing the same interface for both modes. The driver launches Claude Code sessions, sends prompts, and streams events.

## Architecture

### Unified Session Driver Interface
```bash
# Interface functions (implemented differently per driver)
session_driver_start()     # repo_ctx -> session_handle
session_driver_send()      # session, message
session_driver_stream()    # session -> normalized_events
session_driver_interrupt() # session
session_driver_stop()      # session
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event schema:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-08T12:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question|external_prompt",
    "question": {...},
    "status": "success|error"
  }
}
```

## Implementation

### 3.1 Driver Detection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null && ntm --version &>/dev/null; then
        echo "ntm"
    else
        echo "local"
    fi
}
```

### 3.2 Local Driver (tmux + stream-json)
```bash
local_driver_start() {
    local worktree_path="$1"
    local session_id="ru-review-$$-$(basename "$worktree_path")"

    # Create named pipe for output
    local pipe_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/pipes/${session_id}.pipe"
    mkfifo "$pipe_file"

    # Launch Claude Code with stream-json
    tmux new-session -d -s "$session_id" -c "$worktree_path" \
        "claude -p '...' --output-format stream-json 2>&1 | tee $pipe_file"

    echo "$session_id"
}

local_driver_stream() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local pipe_file="$RU_STATE_DIR/pipes/${session_id}.pipe"

    while IFS= read -r line; do
        normalize_claude_event "$line"
    done < "$pipe_file"
}
```

### 3.3 ntm Driver (Robot Mode API)
```bash
ntm_driver_start() {
    local worktree_path="$1"
    local session_name="ru-review-$$"

    ntm --robot-spawn "$session_name" \
        --cc=1 \
        --working-dir="$worktree_path"
}

ntm_driver_stream() {
    local session_id="$1"
    ntm --robot-stream="$session_id" --format=json
}

ntm_driver_send() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
    ntm --robot-send="$session_id" --msg="$message"
}
```

### 3.4 Prompt Chunking
tmux has ~4KB practical limit per SendKeys:
```bash
ntm_send_prompt_chunked() {
    local session="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local chunk_size=3500

    local offset=0 length=${#prompt}
    while [[ $offset -lt $length ]]; do
        local chunk="${prompt:$offset:$chunk_size}"
        ntm --robot-send="$session" --msg="$chunk"
        ((offset += chunk_size))
        sleep 0.1
    done
}
```

### 3.5 Review Prompt Generation
```bash
generate_review_prompt() {
    local repo_name="$1"
    local worktree_path="$2"
    local run_id="$3"
    local work_items_json="$4"

    cat <<EOF
We don't allow PRs or outside contributions...
[Full policy prompt from EXTENDING doc]

WORK ITEMS TO REVIEW:
$work_items_json

REQUIRED OUTPUT:
You MUST produce .ru/review-plan.json conforming to schema v1.
EOF
}
```

## Unit Tests (scripts/test_unit_session_driver.sh)

1. **test_driver_detection_with_ntm**: Verify ntm detected when available
2. **test_driver_detection_without_ntm**: Verify local fallback
3. **test_local_session_start**: Verify tmux session created
4. **test_local_pipe_creation**: Verify named pipe created
5. **test_event_normalization**: Verify Claude events normalized correctly
6. **test_prompt_chunking**: Verify large prompts chunked at 3500 chars
7. **test_prompt_generation**: Verify prompt includes policy and work items
8. **test_session_cleanup**: Verify cleanup removes pipes and sessions

## E2E Tests (scripts/test_e2e_session_driver.sh)

1. **test_local_driver_full_cycle**:
   - Start session
   - Send prompt
   - Receive events
   - Session completes
   - Cleanup

2. **test_parallel_sessions**:
   - Start 4 sessions simultaneously
   - All produce events
   - No cross-talk between sessions

3. **test_session_interrupt_recovery**:
   - Start session
   - Send interrupt
   - Verify graceful shutdown

## Logging Requirements
- LOG_DEBUG: "Detected driver: $driver"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Starting session $session_id in $worktree_path"
- LOG_INFO: "Session $session_id started"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Event received: $event_type"
- LOG_DEBUG: "Sending prompt (${#prompt} chars, ${chunks} chunks)"
- LOG_WARN: "Session $session_id stalled"
- LOG_ERROR: "Session $session_id failed: $error"

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Driver detection works correctly
- [ ] Local driver uses tmux + stream-json
- [ ] ntm driver uses robot mode API
- [ ] Both drivers emit same event schema
- [ ] Prompt chunking handles large prompts
- [ ] Named pipes cleaned up on exit
- [ ] All 8 unit tests pass
- [ ] All 3 e2e tests pass","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T01:24:34.430875546-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T10:54:59.08123405-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8q3s","depends_on_id":"bd-ai1z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T01:28:52.41238203-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8zt6","title":"Implement stream-json event parsing","description":"# Task: Implement Stream-JSON Event Parsing

## Purpose
Parse Claude Code's stream-json NDJSON output to extract events for programmatic control: session init, tool use, questions, and completion.

## Background: Stream-JSON Format
Claude Code with --output-format stream-json emits one JSON object per line:

### Event Types

#### 1. System Init
```json
{
  "type": "system",
  "subtype": "init",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "tools": ["Read", "Write", "Edit", "Bash", "Glob", "Grep", ...]
}
```

#### 2. Assistant Message (Text)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {"type": "text", "text": "I'll analyze the codebase..."}
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 3. Assistant Message (Tool Use)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_123",
        "name": "Read",
        "input": {"file_path": "/path/to/file"}
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 4. AskUserQuestion Tool (CRITICAL)
```json
{
  "type": "assistant",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_use",
        "id": "toolu_456",
        "name": "AskUserQuestion",
        "input": {
          "questions": [{
            "question": "Should I refactor the entire auth module?",
            "header": "Approach",
            "options": [
              {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only this bug (5 lines)"},
              {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize auth module (200+ lines)"},
              {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority right now"}
            ],
            "multiSelect": false
          }]
        }
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 5. Tool Result
```json
{
  "type": "user",
  "message": {
    "content": [
      {
        "type": "tool_result",
        "tool_use_id": "toolu_123",
        "content": "file contents..."
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

#### 6. Session Result
```json
{
  "type": "result",
  "status": "success",
  "duration_ms": 45000,
  "session_id": "abc123"
}
```

## Implementation

### parse_stream_json_event()
```bash
parse_stream_json_event() {
    local line="$1"
    local -n _event_type=$2
    local -n _event_data=$3
    
    # Validate JSON
    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        _event_type="invalid"
        _event_data="$line"
        return 1
    fi
    
    _event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
    
    case "$_event_type" in
        system)
            local subtype
            subtype=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.subtype // ""')
            if [[ "$subtype" == "init" ]]; then
                _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{session_id, tools}')
            fi
            ;;
        assistant)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content')
            ;;
        user)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content')
            ;;
        result)
            _event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{status, duration_ms, session_id}')
            ;;
        *)
            _event_data="$line"
            ;;
    esac
    
    return 0
}
```

### detect_ask_user_question()
```bash
detect_ask_user_question() {
    local event_data="$1"
    
    # Check if any content block is AskUserQuestion
    echo "$event_data" | jq -e \
        '.[] | select(.type == "tool_use" and .name == "AskUserQuestion")' \
        >/dev/null 2>&1
}
```

### extract_question_info()
```bash
extract_question_info() {
    local event_data="$1"
    
    # Extract the AskUserQuestion input
    local question_input
    question_input=$(echo "$event_data" | jq -c \
        '.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion") | .input')
    
    # Parse first question (usually only one)
    local question header options multi_select
    question=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].question // ""')
    header=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].header // ""')
    options=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -c '.questions[0].options // []')
    multi_select=$(echo "$question_input" | jq -r '.questions[0].multiSelect // false')
    
    # Format for queue
    cat << EOF
{
  "question": $(echo "$question" | jq -R .),
  "header": $(echo "$header" | jq -R .),
  "options": $options,
  "multi_select": $multi_select,
  "tool_use_id": $(echo "$event_data" | jq '.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion") | .id')
}
EOF
}
```

### detect_text_question()
Check for questions in plain text (fallback):
```bash
detect_text_question() {
    local text="$1"
    
    # Question patterns
    local -a patterns=(
        'Should I'
        'Do you want'
        'Would you like'
        'Please confirm'
        'Choose.*:'
        'Which.*\?'
        'What.*\?'
        'How should'
    )
    
    for pattern in "${patterns[@]}"; do
        if echo "$text" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    return 1
}
```

### Stream Processing Loop
```bash
process_stream_json() {
    local log_file="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    
    tail -f "$log_file" | while IFS= read -r line; do
        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
        
        local event_type event_data
        if ! parse_stream_json_event "$line" event_type event_data; then
            continue
        fi
        
        case "$event_type" in
            system)
                $callback "init" "$event_data"
                ;;
            assistant)
                if detect_ask_user_question "$event_data"; then
                    local question_info
                    question_info=$(extract_question_info "$event_data")
                    $callback "question" "$question_info"
                fi
                ;;
            result)
                $callback "complete" "$event_data"
                break
                ;;
        esac
    done
}
```

## Edge Cases
- Malformed JSON lines (skip with warning)
- Multiple tool_use in single message
- Nested questions (multiple questions array)
- Very long text content (truncate for display)
- Binary content in tool results

## Testing
- Parse each event type correctly
- Detect AskUserQuestion
- Extract question details
- Handle malformed input gracefully
- Text question detection works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All event types parsed correctly
- [ ] AskUserQuestion detected reliably
- [ ] Question info extracted with all fields
- [ ] Text question fallback works
- [ ] Malformed input handled gracefully
- [ ] Stream processing loop works with tail -f","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:35:14.824939202-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:49:58.265521042-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:49:58.265521042-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented stream-json event parsing functions and 14 unit tests, all passing","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8zt6","depends_on_id":"bd-9s7y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.815511851-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-9","title":"Phase 9: Repo List Management","description":"**EPIC: Repository List Loading & Management**

## Goal
Implement robust loading, parsing, and deduplication of repository lists from config files.

## Rationale
Users maintain lists of repos to sync. These lists support comments, blank lines, branch pinning (repo@branch), and custom local names (repo as custom-name). We must parse all this robustly and handle edge cases.

## List File Format
```
# Comments start with #
# Blank lines are ignored

https://github.com/owner/repo
owner/repo
owner/repo@develop
owner/repo as custom-name
```

## Deduplication
Multiple list files may reference the same repo. We dedupe by resolved local path, keeping the first occurrence. This prevents double-processing.

## Future: Tags
We're reserving syntax for tags: `owner/repo #tag=backend`. Not implementing now, but the parser should not break on this.

## Success Criteria
- List files parse correctly with all syntax variants
- Comments and blank lines are skipped
- Duplicates are detected and deduplicated
